discussion_comments.comment_body:
As enum namespace is introduced in cpp11, which is optional in ncnn, we use XXXX_YYYY enum name to indicate the scope semantic. See exsamples in binaryop, eltwise etc

```
Interpolation_BILINEAR
Padding_ZEROS
```

+
+namespace ncnn {
+
+class GridSample : public Layer
+{
+public:

+public:

+    int resize_type;  // 1=bilinear  2=nearest  3=bicubic

+

discussion_comments.comment_body: Consider not using Chinese Pinyin when naming a variable.

commented_code:
#define NCNN_LOGE(...)
 #endif
 
+#ifdef _MSC_VER
+    #define forceinline __forceinline
+#elif defined(__GNUC__)
+    #define forceinline inline __attribute__((__always_inline__))
+#elif defined(__CLANG__)
+    #if __has_attribute(__always_inline__)
+        #define forceinline inline __attribute__((__always_inline__))
+    #else
+        #define forceinline inline
+    #endif
+#else
+    #define forceinline inline
+#endif
+

discussion_comments.comment_body:
the forceinline name may conflict with other third-party libraries or user-defined macros
what about NCNN_FORCEINLINE ?

discussion_comments.comment_body: added NCNN_FORCE_INLINE option in the latest commit
please do not prefix with underscore for attribute identifier
